findings.title: Sensitive Credentials Stored in ConfigMap
findings.description: Task 1 specifies storing PostgreSQL, Redis, and S3 credentials in a Kubernetes ConfigMap ('sigma1-infra-endpoints'). ConfigMaps are stored in plain text and are not suitable for sensitive secrets, exposing the entire infrastructure to privilege escalation if the namespace is compromised.
findings.recommendation: Modify Task 1 to use Kubernetes Secrets instead of ConfigMaps for all credentials. For the production scale tasks, introduce a secret management solution like External Secrets Operator or HashiCorp Vault.

findings.title: Missing API Authentication and RBAC
findings.description: The plan details creating administrative and financial endpoints (e.g., POST /api/v1/catalog/products, POST /api/v1/invoices, POST /api/v1/equipment-api/checkout) without any mention of authentication mechanisms (e.g., JWT, OAuth) or Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
findings.recommendation: Add a dedicated task to implement an Identity Provider (IdP) integration. Update Tasks 2-6 to include authentication middleware and enforce strict RBAC policies on all state-mutating and administrative endpoints.

findings.title: Unspecified File Upload Sanitization
findings.description: Task 3 involves integrating S3/R2 for product images. There are no explicit requirements for validating file types, sizes, or sanitizing uploads, which could lead to malicious file hosting or denial of service via oversized uploads.
findings.recommendation: Update Task 3 to explicitly require strict MIME-type validation (allowlisting only specific image formats), file size limits, and randomized renaming of uploaded files before storing them in S3/R2.

findings.title: Lack of Security Audit Logging
findings.description: While general observability (OTel, Jaeger) is planned in the scale tasks, there is no explicit requirement for immutable audit logging of sensitive business operations such as invoice creation, inventory adjustments, or lead scoring.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured security audit logging in the Rust and Go services (Tasks 4, 5, 6) to record the actor identity, action, timestamp, and resource ID for all critical state-mutating requests.

findings.title: Missing Container Image Scanning
findings.description: The plan specifies base images (e.g., valkey/valkey:7.2-alpine) and custom service deployments but does not include a task for automated container image vulnerability scanning.
findings.recommendation: Add a subtask to the CI/CD pipeline setup to integrate a container scanning tool (like Trivy or Clair) to block deployments of images containing known critical vulnerabilities.

summary: The current task plan establishes a solid functional baseline but contains critical security gaps in its initial phases. The most pressing issues are the storage of infrastructure credentials in plain-text ConfigMaps and the complete absence of authentication and authorization mechanisms for administrative APIs. Addressing these foundational flaws before implementation begins is crucial to prevent immediate compromise.
strengths: Proactive inclusion of Kubernetes Network Policies (Task 13) for defense-in-depth and lateral movement restriction.
Integration of static analysis and vulnerability auditing tools (cargo audit, go vet, staticcheck) directly into the test strategies.
Comprehensive observability stack planned (OpenTelemetry, Jaeger) which will significantly aid in incident response and anomaly detection.
TLS termination and certificate management via cert-manager is explicitly planned for all external-facing services.
